_meta.what_this_is: Example input for run_local.py. Copy this file, replace every number with YOUR own point-of-care figures, then run:  python3 run_local.py --input my_data.json
_meta.important: These values are ILLUSTRATIVE placeholders, not real surveillance data. Your filled-in data stays in your fork; run_local.py makes no network calls and writes nothing outside your machine.
_meta.licensing: CC-BY-4.0 covers this schema and template. Your own figures are yours.
_meta.horizon_days_note: horizon_days must be 7, 14, or 30 (the model's validated look-ahead windows).
_meta.optional_fields_note: The four fields below the required block (history, case_definition_version, transmission_priors_override, model_basis_note) are optional. Omit them and the runner behaves exactly as before. Supplying them lets you exploit information the public method does not have: prior snapshots, a declared case definition, and a measured BDBV serial-interval or R prior fitted to your own line list. See FORKING.md.

source_zones.note: ILLUSTRATIVE - your observed counts for this affected zone

corridor_edge_weights._help: Relative movement/transport intensity for each 'source->target' corridor. 1.0 (the default for any corridor you omit) means no information. Raise corridors you know carry more travel. This is the biggest corridor-discrimination lever after per-zone case counts. Keys without '->' (like this one) are ignored.

history._help: Optional. Each entry is a prior snapshot of the SAME outbreak in the same source_zones schema. The earliest as_of in history (vs. the top-level as_of) tightens the visibility nowcast: the cadence between snapshots replaces the conservative 7-day default. Two prior snapshots are usually enough to switch the visibility method_basis from single_snapshot to empirical_history.

transmission_priors_override._help: Optional. Drop in a measured BDBV serial-interval or R prior fitted to YOUR own line-list cadence to replace the species-matched default (Wamala 2010 + MacNeil 2010 + Albariño 2013, see lovs/lovs_priors_bundibugyo.py). Any field omitted falls back to the default. r_prior_gamma and serial_interval_gamma are (alpha, beta) shape-rate gammas; under_ascertainment_uniform is (lo, hi); incubation_gamma is (alpha, beta). species and notes are free-form strings carried through to the model audit trail.
transmission_priors_override.notes: ILLUSTRATIVE override. Replace with your own fitted values.
